summary: The diff addresses both REQ-1 (backend Motoko registration fix) and REQ-2 (frontend registration flow fix). The backend registerUser function now correctly returns a discriminated union result instead of trapping. The frontend Registration.tsx and useQueries.ts have been updated to handle errors, show meaningful messages, disable the button during submission, and redirect on success. One concern: the owner username block is enforced only on the frontend, not in the backend registerUser function, which was an explicit AC item for REQ-1. Additionally, useQueries.ts uses a non-standard result.__kind__ check on the discriminated union rather than standard Candid variant pattern matching.

missing_requirements.requirement: The owner username 'Akgamer4354' remains blocked from registration (backend enforcement)
missing_requirements.reason: The backend registerUser function in main.mo does not contain any check blocking the username 'Akgamer4354'. The block only exists in Registration.tsx on the frontend, meaning a direct canister call could register the owner username.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration module (migration.mo) was added that transforms old UserCredential records to new ones with a different shape, along with a migration hook in the actor
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked to fix registration logic and the frontend flow. A data migration module was not requested in either REQ-1 or REQ-2.
